queries: Introduction to Apache Airflow E2E pipeline 2024
action.query: Introduction to Apache Airflow E2E pipeline 2024

annotations.title: Introduction to Apache Airflow®: A Technical Overview for Beginners | Astronomer Documentation
Building a Simple Data Pipeline — Airflow 3.1.8 Documentation
Introduction to Apache Airflow®: A Technical Overview for Beginners | Astronomer Documentation
Getting Started with Apache Airflow | DataCamp

GitHub - airscholar/e2e-data-engineering: An end-to-end data engineering pipeline that orchestrates data ingestion, processing, and storage using Apache Airflow, Python, Apache Kafka, Apache Zookeeper, Apache Spark, and Cassandra. All components are containerized with Docker for easy deployment and scalability.

content.text:
Apache Airflow is an open-source platform designed for programmatically authoring, scheduling, and monitoring workflows, particularly data pipelines. It enables users to define workflows as code, facilitating dynamic and scalable orchestration of complex tasks. ([astronomer.io](https://www.astronomer.io/docs/learn/intro-to-airflow/?utm_source=openai))

To build an end-to-end (E2E) pipeline with Apache Airflow, you can follow the official tutorial provided in the Airflow documentation. This guide walks you through creating a simple data pipeline, including tasks such as data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL), and provides insights into managing task dependencies and scheduling. ([airflow.apache.org](https://airflow.apache.org/docs/apache-airflow/stable/tutorial/pipeline.html?utm_source=openai))

For a more comprehensive understanding, consider exploring the "Introduction to Apache Airflow" tutorial by Astronomer. This resource offers a technical overview of Airflow, covering its core concepts, common use cases, and guidance on running Airflow, making it suitable for beginners seeking a solid foundation. ([astronomer.io](https://www.astronomer.io/docs/learn/intro-to-airflow/?utm_source=openai))

Additionally, the "Getting Started with Apache Airflow" tutorial by DataCamp provides an interactive approach to learning Airflow. It guides you through the installation and configuration process, leading up to writing your first Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG), which is fundamental for orchestrating workflows in Airflow. ([datacamp.com](https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/getting-started-with-apache-airflow?utm_source=openai))

For practical examples and advanced use cases, the "End-to-End Data Engineering Pipeline" project on GitHub demonstrates how to orchestrate data ingestion, processing, and storage using Apache Airflow, along with other technologies like Apache Kafka, Apache Spark, and Cassandra. This project is containerized with Docker, facilitating easy deployment and scalability. ([github.com](https://github.com/airscholar/e2e-data-engineering?utm_source=openai))

These resources should provide a solid foundation for understanding and implementing end-to-end pipelines with Apache Airflow.
